title: Shortcut for most recent container
body:
It is desirable to have a shortcut for the most
recently created container.  We can now use "**latest"
to represent the most recent container instead of its
container ID or name.  For example:

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: Not sure how much of this is a developers/testers request.  Will people using podman in the real world care.  Not that I am against it.  But it is weird syntax.  Can you give me an example of another tool that would do something like this?

I think initially this is initially more oriented towards developers and possibly scripted actions.  It clearly makes some things easier on the command line that fetching a container ID.  Bash has things like this to help users refer to the last arg in a previous command for example.  And in some regards, this parallels docker's use of -l|--latest in docker ps.
But this would be racy. Since if If cri-o started a container after you did, you would not get the last container you started, but the last container started in libpod.
i'm ok with that ... we cant have it both ways.
It's useful for cases when you only have podman running. And for scripting the integration tests. I'm not in love with the **latest sigil, but otherwise this seems like a solid addition
Well it needs to be documented in the man page and explained it shortcomings as a race condition.
which man page would you like it in? i would prefer not to slew it all over each subcommand man page.
Any command that can use **latest should mention it in the man page. Otherwise as a user how would you know it exists. You should also mention it in the help sections.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably b231e34) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

Fix Tom's Nits and then you can merge, although I am not overlay crazy about this altogether, because of Racyness and the difficulty in defining --latest (Latest Modified, Created)  Latest changes if someone does a rm and rmi....
